meta.title: One Chain to Rule Them All: Can Solana Alone Support Wall Street's Demands?

transcript:
Matthew Hougan: Welcome everybody. This is going to be really exciting. We have 14 minutes and 48 seconds to decide if Solana alone can support all of Wall Street demands. Max, can you give a 10-second introduction to yourself?

Max Resnick: Hi everyone, I'm Max. I work at Anza. We work on the Agave client, building Solana, making it IBRL, making it ready for internet capital markets.

Matthew Hougan: Amazing. Ren, why don't you introduce yourself?

Ren CF: Hi, I'm Ren. I'm a General Partner at Electric Capital. We've been long-time investors in Solana supporting the ecosystem... and excited here to play devil's advocate to Max.

Matthew Hougan: All right, we're going to run this as a traditional debate. Two-minute opening statements. Max, we'll start with you.

Max Resnick: Can Solana support all of Wall Street's demands today? I'm going to argue yes. What are those demands? Scale, market structure, and regulatory clarity. On scale, we've made tremendous strides... We actually had 100,000 transactions per second on mainnet, in production this year. Market structure... prop AMMs are evolving in real time, providing tighter spreads, doing 3x the volume of Binance on SOL/USD right now. On clarity, we have Nasdaq listed securities, Class A securities, on-chain on Solana today through people like Superstate. But is current Wall Street infrastructure really ready? To issue a security, you hire underwriters, counsel, regulators, central depositories, custodians... it's complex. Trading has counterparty risk. Settlement is T+1 day. On Solana, you issue, trade, and settle on Solana in T+1 millisecond, not T+1 day.

Matthew Hougan: You have the floor, Ren.

Ren CF: Thank you. I have four main themes. First, Solana today is not ready. Peak message demands on Nasdaq (millions per second) dwarf Solana's current TPS budget. Second, security programs are opaque to institutions; audits are pre-deployment. Third, legal finality versus cryptographic finality creates tension for error corrections. Fourth, Wall Street is distributed globally; laws of physics make a single-leader network challenging for global latency. We need to onboard digital asset treasuries first before the rest of TradFi.

Matthew Hougan: Max, what do you say about a million transactions per second?

Max Resnick: That's a consequence of very bad financial market design in traditional finance... central limit order books. We have on-chain proprietary AMMs where a single oracle update does the equivalent of hundreds of transactions. Traditional structures have price-time priority which creates inflated transaction demand. I think today we can handle a lot of trades. We're going to have P-token early next year, creating even more trades.

Matthew Hougan: Ren, is Wall Street even the right target?

Ren CF: Just a counterpoint, most private AMMs today just take Binance's mid-price... weak alpha. I think the opportunity for Solana is doing things the current system cannot, like helping small to mid-sized cross-border businesses access capital. That's a tremendous opportunity.

Matthew Hougan: Max, what about the argument that putting it all on one chain is too much?

Max Resnick: If you put it on Ethereum, it would fall over. Modular designs create tension and risk when you hook components together. Even if components work well individually, they aren't designed to work together seamlessly like a single chain.

Matthew Hougan: Ren, close your eyes and imagine the future state. One chain or many?

Ren CF: probably 90% will happen on one chain, but on a generational timescale. The real world is complicated, and those complexities melt into the software. While one chain is economically efficient, regional and regulatory realities might prevent total consolidation.

Matthew Hougan: Max, what does the future look like?

Max Resnick: I see Solana everywhere. Information flowing all over the world into this one place. The only thing that breaks that is us, if we don't ship and educate.

Matthew Hougan: Closing thoughts. SEC chair says all stocks on chain in a few years. Will Solana be ready?

Max Resnick: We are ready today. It's permissionless. If it doesn't have what you want, you can build it.

Ren CF: Solana is the leading contender, but crypto has lost its edge recently. I'm personally waiting for Firedancer and multiple concurrent leaders next year. It's important to keep a Day 1 mindset.

# Solana Breakpoint 2025: Key Announcements and Stories from Saturday
Day Three maintained the high expectations with announcements from Solflare, Solana Mobile, and Colosseum, among others.
[Pablo](https://solanafloor.com/author/pablo)
Copy link
* Published:
* Edited:
It was said that this year’s Breakpoint would be Solana’s biggest conference yet, and I’m sure none of the several thousand delegates that made their way to the Etihad Arena this week would disagree with that sentiment.
Saturday’s session featured a diverse set of updates highlighting Solana’s continued expansion across hardware, real-world assets, and institutional finance, as well as side events such as Predictify, hosted by[Kalshi](https://x.com/Kalshi?s=20).
> [](https://twitter.com/SolanaFloor/status/1999766576091025438?s=20)> The last day of Breakpoint 2025 delivered a mix of hackathon results, product demonstrations, and strategic commitments that underscored the network’s growing role as a settlement layer for both digital and traditional assets.
Here’s what you missed if you’re not in Abu Dhabi:
## At Breakpoint
1. Colosseum has announced winners for its Cypherpunk hackathon
* *Unruggable, the Solana-native hardware wallet, is crowned grand champion.*
> [](https://twitter.com/SolanaFloor/status/1999765402453467212?s=20)> 1. Uranium Digital demoed their product, which enables institutions to buy Uranium onchain for the first time.
* *Settlement is happening fully on Solana*
* *The entire crypto experience is abstracted*
> [](https://twitter.com/SolanaFloor/status/1999744679441826044?s=20)> 1. RockawayX launches 3 vaults on Solana.
* *Risk-curated RWA vault on Kamino*
* *Additional vaults on Exponent Finance and MidasRWA*
* *Partners with Figure, Onre, Solstice, and Huma as professional curators for these vaults.*
> [](https://twitter.com/SolanaFloor/status/1999753859456204968?s=20)> 1. Solana Mobile is expanding beyond its own devices to additional hardware.
* *Working with FXTech, MediaTek, and Trustonic to integrate the Solana Mobile stack at the chipset level for other Android devices.*
* *MediaTek ships roughly 50% of all Android devices globally.*
> [](https://twitter.com/SolanaFloor/status/1999757725505642967?s=20)> 1. Splyce Finance introduces $SFULC on Solana.
* *$SFULC is a yield-bearing token offering 10% APY powered by Fulcrum Lending*
* *Powered by real cash flows from senior, secured multifamily credit.*
* *$SFULC can be minted with stablecoins or bought directly on Raydium.*
> [](https://twitter.com/SolanaFloor/status/1999761140931526825?s=20)> 1. VC Firm 6th Man Ventures announces that it is focused on Solan

sources.title: Sonami Launches First Layer 2 Token on Solana to Ensure Transaction Efficiency and End Congestion Spikes
sources.snippet:
Sonami Launches First Layer 2 Token on Solana to Ensure Transaction Efficiency and End Congestion Spikes | Currency News | Financial and Business News | Markets Insider![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)
[Business Insider](https://www.businessinsider.com/)
[My Markets Watchlist](https://markets.businessinsider.com/mymarkets?originurl=/)
[Markets](https://markets.businessinsider.com/)[News](https://markets.businessinsider.com/news)
# Sonami Launches First Layer 2 Token on Solana to Ensure Transaction Efficiency and End Congestion Spikes
Chainwire
Dec. 8, 2025, 03:38 AM
![Featured Image](https://app.chainwire.org/storage/uploads/users/1200_x_720_new_1765185659iq3IJd3493.jpg)
**New York, United States, December 8th, 2025, Chainwire**
**$SNMI Pioneers Transaction Bundling Technology to Future-Proof Solana Ecosystem Against Peak Demand**
Sonami ($SNMI), the first Layer 2 token built on the Solana blockchain, today announced its mission to dramatically enhance network reliability and transaction efficiency during periods of high demand. Sonami's solution leverages cutting-edge Layer 2 transaction bundling to minimize congestion and support the continued growth of high-frequency decentralized applications (dApps) on Solana.
The move addresses a critical scaling challenge: while Solana is celebrated for its speed, the network can experience congestion during moments of high activity, such as meme coin surges, NFT mints, or rapid-fire decentralized trading. These episodes, while signaling strong user interest, can stress the network, leading to delayed transactions and inconsistent user experiences.
Sonami aims to solve this by evolving the scaling architecture. Its Layer 2 network uses**transaction bundling**to intelligently group multiple user interactions into a single, optimized transaction that is ultimately processed on Solana鈥檚 Layer 1. This significantly reduces the network load, ensuring scalability without compromising the base chain鈥檚 speed or security.
> "The expectation in Web3 is quickly shifting from 'fast most of the time' to 'fast all the time'," said **> Zakit Mobad, founder of Sonami Foundation
**> . "Sonami is committed to being the performance multiplier that unlocks Solana鈥檚 full potential. By integrating a Layer 2 solution, we ensure developers can build more ambitious real-time apps and users can enjoy a seamless experience, even during periods of high market volatility."
**A Stronger Ecosystem for Next-Gen dApps**
The project confirms its focus on real-world use cases where split-second interactions matter most, including:
* Real-time multiplayer gaming.
* High-volume decentralized trading.
* Microtransaction-powered utility applications.
**Growth and Roadmap**
The Sonami presale phase continues, signaling proactive development. The project is focusing on its upcoming**Token Generation Event (TGE)**and subsequent planned listings on both decentralized and centralized exchanges (DEX/CEX) after the presale concludes. This expansion ensures the ecosystem is resilient and ready for its next era of mainstream adoption.
**About Sonami ($SNMI)**
Sonami is a pioneering Layer 2 project built on the Solana blockchain, driven by a collective of seasoned blockchain developers and ecosystem architects. The team is united by a shared vision of solving scalability challenges at the protocol level. Sonami鈥檚 core mission is to enhance Solana's transaction efficiency and reliability, ensuring the network remains resilient, capable, and ready for its mainstream future.
Website | X | Instagram | Telegram
##### Contact
**David Dylan**

tags.impact_reason: Debate on Solana's capacity to support Wall Street demands highlights institutional interest and potential but also acknowledges current limitations.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a futuristic, glowing stock ticker tape as the Semantic Anchor because it directly merges the two worlds at the heart of the story: the iconic data stream of Wall Street (the ticker) and the high-speed, futuristic technology of the Solana blockchain. The core conflict is about transaction capacity ('TPS'), which the tape visualizes as a physical, high-velocity flow of information. I included the text '100K TPS' diegetically on the tape itself, as this specific number is the central performance claim being debated and fits naturally into a stream of financial data.
visual_idea.description: A single, impossibly long stock ticker tape made of holographic vellum glows with a vibrant cyan and magenta light. It snakes chaotically through the frame, suspended in a cavernous, dark space reminiscent of a stock exchange's server farm. The tape streams abstract financial symbols, and for a brief moment, the glowing, diegetic characters '100K TPS' are clearly visible as part of the data flow. In the deep, out-of-focus background, endless rows of server racks with faint indicator lights recede into the darkness, suggesting immense scale. Volumetric light shafts cut through the haze, illuminating the tape's path.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A single, impossibly long stock ticker tape, crafted from iridescent holographic vellum, glows intensely with vibrant cyan and magenta light. The tape weaves chaotically and monumentally through a vast, dark, vertical cavernous space, suspended within an awe-inspiring data cathedral. This immense environment evokes a futuristic stock exchange server farm, with towering structures featuring accents of sleek brushed tungsten. The holographic vellum tape continuously streams intricate, abstract financial symbols, and at a pivotal moment, the glowing, diegetic characters '100K TPS' are vividly apparent, integrated directly into the data flow. In the deep, ethereal, and out-of-focus background, endless rows of server racks, constructed from dark, robust materials with subtle brushed tungsten details, recede infinitely into the darkness, their faint indicator lights twinkling like distant stars, emphasizing an overwhelming sense of scale and height. Powerful volumetric light shafts dramatically pierce through a pervasive haze, intensely illuminating the intricate path of the holographic tape. Composed for a vertical 3:4 aspect ratio, emphasizing epic height and monumental scale, with significant negative space at the top to convey grandeur. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, ultra-photorealistic, cinematic, high dynamic range, realistic materials.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a futuristic, glowing stock ticker tape as the Semantic Anchor because it directly merges the two worlds at the heart of the story: the iconic data stream of Wall Street (the ticker) and the high-speed, futuristic technology of the Solana blockchain. The core conflict is about transaction capacity ('TPS'), which the tape visualizes as a physical, high-velocity flow of information. I included the text '100K TPS' diegetically on the tape itself, as this specific number is the central performance claim being debated and fits naturally into a stream of financial data.
visual_idea.description: An extreme macro shot focusing on a sharp bend in the holographic ticker tape. The internal light refracts through its crystalline polymer structure, creating complex caustics and revealing intricate, layered micro-circuitry just beneath its surface. The surface is alive with flowing, abstract glyphs and data patterns that shimmer and shift, suggesting an immense velocity of information transfer. The background is a clean, dark gradient, emphasizing the texture and luminescence of the tape itself.

final_prompt.prompt_text: An extreme macro shot, perfectly centered and symmetrical, filling the 1:1 frame with a sharp bend in a holographic ticker tape. The tape's surface glows with the iridescent, translucent qualities of holographic vellum, its crystalline polymer structure powerfully refracting internal light, generating complex, intricate caustics that dance across its form. Just beneath this shimmering surface, highly detailed, layered micro-circuitry, with subtle brushed tungsten-like metallic textures, is revealed. The tape's surface is alive with flowing, abstract glyphs and intricate data patterns that shimmer, shift, and pulsate, conveying an immense velocity of information transfer. Volumetric lighting emphasizes the depth and internal luminescence, with subtle light rays catching the edges and contributing to a 'data cathedral' aesthetic of grand, intricate digital information. The background is a clean, dark gradient, ensuring intense focus on the tape's texture, luminosity, and high-fidelity detail. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, photorealistic, cinematic.

editorial.headline: Anza Bets on 'Pinocchio' Token Standard to Unlock 100,000 TPS for Wall Street
editorial.standfirst: Anza's Max Resnick claims the new zero-copy protocol will solve Solana's capacity constraints by early next year, but Electric Capital warns that physics鈥攏ot throughput鈥攔emains the ultimate institutional bottleneck.
key_takeaways: **P-token (Pinocchio):** A new Anza-developed token standard launching early next year promises 95% compute efficiency gains, theoretically enabling 100,000 transfers per second.

**The Physics Barrier:** Electric Capital argues a single-leader network cannot satisfy global high-frequency trading due to speed-of-light latency, necessitating multi-leader architectures like **Firedancer**.
**Market Structure Flip:** Anza data shows proprietary AMMs on Solana now handle 3x Binance鈥檚 volume on SOL/USD pairs, challenging the necessity of traditional Central Limit Order Books (CLOBs).
editorial.article_body_markdown:
The debate over Solana鈥檚 institutional readiness has shifted from theoretical throughput to hard engineering reality. At Breakpoint 2025, **Anza** Lead Economist Max Resnick revealed the ecosystem's next major efficiency unlock: the **P-token** (Pinocchio) standard. Scheduled for deployment early next year, this protocol upgrade utilizes a zero-copy, heapless library to reduce Compute Unit (CU) usage by approximately 95% compared to the current SPL Token program. If successful, this would allow the network to validate up to 100,000 token transfers per second鈥攃onverting last August鈥檚 "noop" stress-test figures into viable production capacity for institutional flows.

However, raw throughput may not be enough to court the Nasdaq. **Electric Capital** General Partner Ren CF, playing the role of institutional realist, argued that Solana鈥檚 current single-leader architecture hits a hard ceiling imposed by the laws of physics. For global market makers, the latency required to propagate signals to a single leader node creates arbitrage risks that traditional finance (TradFi) cannot tolerate. Ren contends that true Wall Street adoption requires the implementation of **multiple concurrent leaders**鈥攁 feature anticipated in the upcoming **Firedancer** client鈥攖o overcome these geographic latency penalties.

Beyond infrastructure, the panel highlighted a fundamental clash in market structure philosophy. While TradFi relies on Central Limit Order Books (CLOBs) that generate millions of messages per second, Resnick argued this model is inefficient for blockchain settlement. He pointed to on-chain proprietary Automated Market Makers (AMMs) that are already executing 3x the volume of **Binance** on SOL/USD pairs with significantly fewer messages. The implication is that Wall Street鈥檚 "demands" for millions of TPS might be a symptom of legacy design rather than a requirement for future efficiency. As the ecosystem moves toward T+1 millisecond settlement, the burden of proof now lies on **Anza** to ship Pinocchio and prove that a single chain can indeed swallow the world's liquidity.
